visit_tasks_note:
On assessment, the right little toe wound appeared deteriorated, with necrotic tissue present to the nail bed and surrounding inflammation noted. Due to the wound condition, urgent medical review was indicated.
Wound care was provided prior to transfer. The area was cleansed with Prontosan. Aquacel Ag was applied to the wound bed, covered with sterile gauze, and secured with a Mepore dressing. The client tolerated the procedure well.
The client’s son was contacted, informed of the situation, and attended promptly. The client was taken to hospital for urgent medical assessment and management.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_tasks_note:
I assessed the client and observed severe bilateral lower limb skin breakdown, with extensive erythema, inflammation, and raw excoriated areas involving the lower legs and feet. Several areas were open and weeping, placing the client at high risk of infection and further deterioration. The presentation was assessed as high risk and unsuitable for ongoing community-based management.
Due to the severity of the condition, I contacted Tegan in Gosford office  and requested an ambulance for urgent hospital transfer. Basic protective dressings were applied in the presence of ambulance crew to cover exposed areas and reduce the risk of contamination. I remained with the client, providing reassurance and monitoring, until the ambulance arrived.
I provided a verbal clinical handover to paramedics outlining my assessment findings, wound severity, and care provided. The client was transferred to hospital for urgent medical review and specialist wound management.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_tasks_note: Wound care was attended as per the current care plan. The site was cleansed with Prontosan and gently dried. Atrauman was applied as the contact layer, followed by Sofban padding and Zetuvit for absorption. The dressing was secured with a crepe bandage.  Previously removed dressings were noted to be damp with greenish discolouration and were reviewed as requested by the client. No active bleeding was observed at the time of assessment. The client tolerated the procedure well. The greenish discolouration will be monitored closely at the next scheduled visit on Wednesday, and the client was informed and is happy with this plan. Ongoing wound management to continue as per the care plan.

visit_tasks_note:
General assessment done.
Stable in mood, reasonable in cognition, 
dual continent and independent in ADLs with limitation due to physical decline.
Issues:
-  poor vision in R eye.
-  poor sleep pattern due to pain.
-  mobility limited due to pain in hip, knee, shoulder (all in right side).   He is right handed person.  Managed by Physio every fortnight.
Following advice given:
-  to see GP for better pain management.  Taking only Panadol Osteo.
-  to monitor R lower leg and foot.  It has mild oedema and non- pitting.  Explained the role of chronic kidney disease in fluids build up in legs.  To elevate if in prolong sitting or lying down.
-  to be mindful of falls hazard like clutter and carpet.  
Frank thanked the service provided.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_rate: Assistance with self-care and activities of daily living - Standard

visit_tasks_note:
Wound care attended.  Slight breakdown noted due to mild maceration.
Applied Inadine and island dressing secured with retention tape.  
Eleuphrat cream applied to both legs.  Lower leg skin showed improvement.  Nil rashes and well moisturised.
Socks refitted.
Malcolm stable and happily thanked the service.  Requested to be attended next Friday at lunchtime.  Planning to play bowls after wound care.

visit_tasks_note:
Lengthy discussion of wound care done.
The current wound care plan has been ongoing for months with no progress of healing.
Rob agreed to the following:
Trial of Algisite as primary dressing due to its high absorbency and ability to form a protective, non-adherent gel upon contact with exudate.   
Absorbent pads not to cover the whole of feet as maceration will expand.  To cover only with absorbent pads the affected areas and allow extra fluid to drip onto a towel.  Than other healthy skin to come into long contact with oversoaked pad.
If trial fails, suggested to see a wound specialist for a suitable dressing materials.
Some clustered rashes noted below both knees.  ?? Eczema.
Rob thanked the service with request to inform RN Lal to update.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_tasks_note:
On assessment today, bilateral lower limbs were noted to have significant skin deterioration compared to previous visits. Marked erythema, widespread inflammation, skin breakdown, weeping areas, and crusting were observed, more pronounced around the ankle and foot region. Skin appears very fragile, excoriated, and at high risk of further breakdown and infection. Increased redness and moisture indicate worsening dermatitis skin integrity issues, requiring urgent and consistent management.

 The affected area was cleansed with Prontosan and gently dried. Atrauman was applied as the primary contact layer, followed by Sofban padding. Zetuvit was applied to manage exudate, and the dressing was secured with a crepe bandage. Tubigrip was applied for support and to maintain dressing placement. The client tolerated the procedure well with no immediate concerns noted. Ongoing monitoring to continue as planned.

Due to the extent of skin involvement and exudate, frequent and timely dressing changes are essential to maintain skin integrity, manage moisture, and reduce the risk of infection.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_tasks_note:
Arrived at Petra’s and got no answer when I knocked on door and called out. 
Called Michelle in office who called Petra who had been asleep in the bedroom 
We sat chatting for a while before driving to the tobacconist and then to East Gosford shops. Then to a food bank place in Gosford which wasn’t open. 
We then went to Coles. I drove Petra home and we chatted a while longer until time for me to leave
